prose: **Ferrocene Fukui reactivity analysis** — Property: Fukui reactivity indices (electrophilic f\+: Mulliken-based GFN2-xTB fukui+ and nucleophilic f\- indices at each atom). Level of theory: GFN2-xTB (semiempirical tight-binding) via xtb. Geometry provenance: user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz` (no optimization; single-point). Solvent: gas phase (no implicit continuum). Charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral state; cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2) and anion (charge –1, multiplicity 2) states also computed for finite-difference. Software: xtb (version as provided by default in chemkit). Convergence and caveats: SCF converged for neutral, cationic, and anionic states; no engine warnings reported; integrity.trustworthy = true. Results: Most electrophilic site is the Fe atom (atom 11) with f\+ = 0.22885456; most nucleophilic site is the same Fe atom (atom 11) with f\- = 0.25938191. CLI invocation: `/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method xtb --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/ferrocene/ferrocene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpo5g7kf6d/live.json`.
